verses.latin_vulgate: Addidit quoque Job, assumens parabolam suam, et dixit :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣סֶף אִ֭יּוֹב שְׂאֵ֥ת מְשָׁל֗וֹ וַיֹּאמַֽר׃
verses.king_james: Moreover Job continued his parable, and said,

verses.latin_vulgate: Vivit Deus, qui abstulit judicium meum,  et Omnipotens, qui ad amaritudinem adduxit animam meam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חַי־אֵ֭ל הֵסִ֣יר מִשְׁפָּטִ֑י וְ֝שַׁדַּ֗י הֵמַ֥ר נַפְשִֽׁי׃
verses.king_james: As God liveth, who hath taken away my judgment; and the Almighty, who hath vexed my soul;

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia donec superest halitus in me,  et spiritus Dei in naribus meis,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּי־כׇל־ע֣וֹד נִשְׁמָתִ֣י בִ֑י וְר֖וּחַ אֱל֣וֹהַּ בְּאַפִּֽי׃
verses.king_james: All the while my breath is in me, and the spirit of God is in my nostrils;

verses.latin_vulgate: non loquentur labia mea iniquitatem,  nec lingua mea meditabitur mendacium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־תְּדַבֵּ֣רְנָה שְׂפָתַ֣י עַוְלָ֑ה וּ֝לְשׁוֹנִ֗י אִם־יֶהְגֶּ֥ה רְמִיָּֽה׃
verses.king_james: My lips shall not speak wickedness, nor my tongue utter deceit.

verses.latin_vulgate: Absit a me ut justos vos esse judicem :  donec deficiam, non recedam ab innocentia mea.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חָלִ֣ילָה לִּי֮ אִם־אַצְדִּ֢יק אֶ֫תְכֶ֥ם עַד־אֶגְוָ֑ע לֹֽא־אָסִ֖יר תֻּמָּתִ֣י מִמֶּֽנִּי׃
verses.king_james: God forbid that I should justify you: till I die I will not remove mine integrity from me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Justificationem meam, quam cœpi tenere, non deseram :  neque enim reprehendit me cor meum in omni vita mea.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּצִדְקָתִ֣י הֶ֭חֱזַקְתִּי וְלֹ֣א אַרְפֶּ֑הָ לֹא־יֶחֱרַ֥ף לְ֝בָבִ֗י מִיָּמָֽי׃
verses.king_james: My righteousness I hold fast, and will not let it go: my heart shall not reproach me so long as I live.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sit ut impius, inimicus meus,  et adversarius meus quasi iniquus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְהִ֣י כְ֭רָשָׁע אֹ֣יְבִ֑י וּמִתְקוֹמְמִ֥י כְעַוָּֽל׃
verses.king_james: Let mine enemy be as the wicked, and he that riseth up against me as the unrighteous.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ est enim spes hypocritæ, si avare rapiat,  et non liberet Deus animam ejus ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י מַה־תִּקְוַ֣ת חָ֭נֵף כִּ֣י יִבְצָ֑ע כִּ֤י יֵ֖שֶׁל אֱל֣וֹהַּ נַפְשֽׁוֹ׃
verses.king_james: For what is the hope of the hypocrite, though he hath gained, when God taketh away his soul?

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid Deus audiet clamorem ejus,  cum venerit super eum angustia ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲֽ֭צַעֲקָתוֹ יִשְׁמַ֥ע ׀ אֵ֑ל כִּֽי־תָב֖וֹא עָלָ֣יו צָרָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Will God hear his cry when trouble cometh upon him?

verses.latin_vulgate: aut poterit in Omnipotente delectari,  et invocare Deum omni tempore ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־עַל־שַׁדַּ֥י יִתְעַנָּ֑ג יִקְרָ֖א אֱל֣וֹהַּ בְּכׇל־עֵֽת׃
verses.king_james: Will he delight himself in the Almighty? will he always call upon God?

verses.latin_vulgate: Docebo vos per manum Dei quæ Omnipotens habeat,  nec abscondam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אוֹרֶ֣ה אֶתְכֶ֣ם בְּיַד־אֵ֑ל אֲשֶׁ֥ר עִם־שַׁ֝דַּ֗י לֹ֣א אֲכַחֵֽד׃
verses.king_james: I will teach you by the hand of God: that which is with the Almighty will I not conceal.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce vos omnes nostis :  et quid sine causa vana loquimini ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵן־אַתֶּ֣ם כֻּלְּכֶ֣ם חֲזִיתֶ֑ם וְלָמָּה־זֶּ֝֗ה הֶ֣בֶל תֶּהְבָּֽלוּ׃
verses.king_james: Behold, all ye yourselves have seen it; why then are ye thus altogether vain?

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc est pars hominis impii apud Deum,  et hæreditas violentorum, quam ob Omnipotente suscipient.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זֶ֤ה ׀ חֵלֶק־אָדָ֖ם רָשָׁ֥ע ׀ עִם־אֵ֑ל וְֽנַחֲלַ֥ת עָ֝רִיצִ֗ים מִשַּׁדַּ֥י יִקָּֽחוּ׃
verses.king_james: This is the portion of a wicked man with God, and the heritage of oppressors, which they shall receive of the Almighty.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si multiplicati fuerint filii ejus, in gladio erunt,  et nepotes ejus non saturabuntur pane :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־יִרְבּ֣וּ בָנָ֣יו לְמוֹ־חָ֑רֶב וְ֝צֶאֱצָאָ֗יו לֹ֣א יִשְׂבְּעוּ־לָֽחֶם׃
verses.king_james: If his children be multiplied, it is for the sword: and his offspring shall not be satisfied with bread.

verses.latin_vulgate: qui reliqui fuerint ex eo sepelientur in interitu,  et viduæ illius non plorabunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׂ֭רִידָו בַּמָּ֣וֶת יִקָּבֵ֑רוּ וְ֝אַלְמְנֹתָ֗יו לֹ֣א תִבְכֶּֽינָה׃
verses.king_james: Those that remain of him shall be buried in death: and his widows shall not weep.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si comportaverit quasi terram argentum,  et sicut lutum præparaverit vestimenta :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־יִצְבֹּ֣ר כֶּעָפָ֣ר כָּ֑סֶף וְ֝כַחֹ֗מֶר יָכִ֥ין מַלְבּֽוּשׁ׃
verses.king_james: Though he heap up silver as the dust, and prepare raiment as the clay;

verses.latin_vulgate: præparabit quidem, sed justus vestietur illis,  et argentum innocens dividet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יָ֭כִין וְצַדִּ֣יק יִלְבָּ֑שׁ וְ֝כֶ֗סֶף נָקִ֥י יַחֲלֹֽק׃
verses.king_james: He may prepare it, but the just shall put it on, and the innocent shall divide the silver.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ædificavit sicut tinea domum suam,  et sicut custos fecit umbraculum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בָּנָ֣ה כָעָ֣שׁ בֵּית֑וֹ וּ֝כְסֻכָּ֗ה עָשָׂ֥ה נֹצֵֽר׃
verses.king_james: He buildeth his house as a moth, and as a booth that the keeper maketh.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dives, cum dormierit, nihil secum auferet :  aperiet oculos suos, et nihil inveniet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עָשִׁ֣יר יִ֭שְׁכַּב וְלֹ֣א יֵאָסֵ֑ף עֵינָ֖יו פָּקַ֣ח וְאֵינֶֽנּוּ׃
verses.king_james: The rich man shall lie down, but he shall not be gathered: he openeth his eyes, and he is not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Apprehendet eum quasi aqua inopia :  nocte opprimet eum tempestas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תַּשִּׂיגֵ֣הוּ כַ֭מַּיִם בַּלָּה֑וֹת לַ֝֗יְלָה גְּנָבַ֥תּוּ סוּפָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Terrors take hold on him as waters, a tempest stealeth him away in the night.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tollet eum ventus urens, et auferet,  et velut turbo rapiet eum de loco suo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִשָּׂאֵ֣הוּ קָדִ֣ים וְיֵלַ֑ךְ וִ֝ישָׂעֲרֵ֗הוּ מִמְּקֹמֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: The east wind carrieth him away, and he departeth: and as a storm hurleth him out of his place.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et mittet super eum, et non parcet :  de manu ejus fugiens fugiet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיַשְׁלֵ֣ךְ עָ֭לָיו וְלֹ֣א יַחְמֹ֑ל מִ֝יָּד֗וֹ בָּר֥וֹחַ יִבְרָֽח׃
verses.king_james: For God shall cast upon him, and not spare: he would fain flee out of his hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Stringet super eum manus suas,  et sibilabit super illum, intuens locum ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִשְׂפֹּ֣ק עָלֵ֣ימוֹ כַפֵּ֑ימוֹ וְיִשְׁרֹ֥ק עָ֝לָ֗יו מִמְּקֹמֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Men shall clap their hands at him, and shall hiss him out of his place.
